seoTitle: Clarence Park vs Clay Wells — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clarence Park and Clay Wells. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clarence Park vs Clay Wells: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clarence Park and Clay Wells.
heading: Clarence Park vs Clay Wells — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clarence Park, South Australia and Clay Wells, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clay Wells has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Clarence Park (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clarence Park or Clay Wells safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clarence Park has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Clay Wells scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clarence Park scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Clay Wells scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clarence Park or Clay Wells?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clay Wells has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
